description: Canonical observation codes for swarm-reasoning. All agents must use codes from this registry. Unknown codes are rejected by the tool layer at write time.

codes.description: The raw text of the claim under investigation, as submitted.
URL of the article or document from which the claim was extracted.
Publication date of the source document. Format: YYYYMMDD.
Subject domain of the claim. Controlled vocabulary: HEALTHCARE, ECONOMICS, POLICY, SCIENCE, ELECTION, CRIME, OTHER.
ClaimBuster-style check-worthiness score. 1.0 = highly check-worthy factual claim. 0.0 = opinion or non-factual statement.
Claim text normalized for matching: lowercased, entity references resolved, hedging language removed.
Named person mentioned in the claim. One OBX row per person. Value is the canonical name.
Named organization mentioned in the claim. One OBX row per organization.
Date or date range referenced in the claim. Format: YYYYMMDD or YYYYMMDD-YYYYMMDD.
Geographic location mentioned in the claim. One OBX row per location.
Numeric claim embedded in text. Value is the extracted quantity and unit. e.g. '87% of adults'.
Whether a ClaimReview match was found. Coded value: TRUE^Match Found^FCK or FALSE^No Match^FCK.
Verdict from the matched ClaimReview entry. Coded as original reviewer's rating system. e.g. FALSE^False^POLITIFACT.
Name of the fact-checking organization that published the matched ClaimReview entry.
URL of the matched ClaimReview fact-check article.
Semantic similarity between submitted claim and matched ClaimReview claim text. Scores below 0.75 are flagged as uncertain matches.
Number of articles covering this claim from this spectrum segment. The agent field identifies which coverage agent wrote the observation.
How this spectrum segment frames the claim. Coded value: SUPPORTIVE^Supportive^FCK, CRITICAL^Critical^FCK, NEUTRAL^Neutral^FCK, ABSENT^Not Covered^FCK.
Name of the highest-credibility source covering the claim in this spectrum segment.
URL of the top coverage source article.
Degree of coverage asymmetry across the political spectrum. 0.0 = uniform coverage. 1.0 = complete blindspot (only one spectrum segment covering).
Which spectrum segment is absent or underrepresented. Coded: LEFT^Left Absent^FCK, RIGHT^Right Absent^FCK, CENTER^Center Absent^FCK, NONE^No Blindspot^FCK.
Whether the claim is covered and consistent across all three spectrum segments. TRUE^Corroborated^FCK or FALSE^Not Corroborated^FCK.
Name of the primary authoritative source consulted. e.g. CDC, SEC, court filing, WHO.
URL of the primary authoritative source document.
Whether primary source evidence supports or contradicts the claim. SUPPORTS^Supports Claim^FCK, CONTRADICTS^Contradicts Claim^FCK, PARTIAL^Partially Supports^FCK, ABSENT^No Evidence Found^FCK.
Confidence in the domain evidence finding. Penalized when source is indirect, dated, or ambiguous.
Final confidence score synthesized from all upstream agent findings. Drives VERDICT mapping per observation-schema-spec.md Section 9.
Final verdict coded value. See observation-schema-spec.md Section 9 for full coded value set and confidence range mapping.
Human-readable explanation of the verdict. References specific upstream findings by OBX sequence number. Max 1000 chars.
Number of upstream F-status OBX rows used as inputs to the synthesis. Low counts indicate insufficient evidence.
If the synthesizer overrides a ClaimReview verdict, the reason is recorded here. Empty if no override occurred.
